title: Banksia altior
taxonomy: Banksia altior is classified within the genus Banksia and the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia and is a part of the Proteales order. Banksia altior is further classified as a member of the subgenus Banksia subg. Banksia. The species is characterized by its unique combination of features, including its flower spikes and leaf morphology. As part of the Banksia genus, it is closely related to other species in the Western Australian flora.

intro: Banksia altior, also known as the tall banksia, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the Proteaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Banksia altior include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Banksia altior include teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
